topics.title: Day 1: SOC Models and Structures
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: SOC Models and Structures is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Understand SOC models
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 1: SOC Models and Structures and its role in SOC architecture, tiers, metrics
topics.interviewQuestion: What are SOC models and structures?
topics.interviewAnswer: Internal SOC (full control, high cost), External MSSP (outsourced, lower cost), Hybrid (internal + MSSP). Virtual SOC (distributed team). Co-managed SOC (shared responsibility). Structure: SOC manager, Tier 1 (triage), Tier 2 (investigation), Tier 3 (hunting/threat research), detection engineering team.

quiz.question: What SOC model has highest control?
What SOC model has lowest cost?
What does Tier 1 do?
What does Tier 3 do?
What is a virtual SOC?
Which best describes Day 1: SOC Models and Structures?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: SOC Models and Structures is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: SOC Models and Structures?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: SOC Models and Structures typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: SOC Models and Structures apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: SOC Models and Structures concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: SOC Models and Structures?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: SOC Models and Structures at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: SOC Models and Structures?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: SOC Models and Structures?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: SOC Models and Structures is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: SOC Models and Structures during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: SOC Models and Structures during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: SOC Models and Structures?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: SOC Models and Structures can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: SOC Models and Structures helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: SOC Models and Structures prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: SOC Models and Structures relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: SOC Models and Structures can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: SOC Processes
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: SOC Processes is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Establish SOC processes
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 2: SOC Processes and its role in SOC architecture, tiers, metrics
topics.interviewAnswer: Alert triage: acknowledge, categorize, prioritize. Escalation: criteria for Tier 2/3, communication channels. Handover: shift change process, active incidents, ongoing investigations. Case management: documentation, evidence, timeline. Communication: internal (IT, management) and external (legal, PR, law enforcement).

quiz.question: What is first triage step?
What does escalation define?
options: Criteria for when to involve higher tiers

quiz.question: What is handover process?
options: Shift change with active incident status

quiz.question: When is law enforcement contacted?
What is SLAs purpose?
Which best describes Day 2: SOC Processes?

topics.title: Day 3: SOC Tools and Stack
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: SOC Tools and Stack is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Build SOC tool stack
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 3: SOC Tools and Stack and its role in SOC architecture, tiers, metrics
topics.interviewQuestion: What tools does a SOC need?
topics.interviewAnswer: SIEM (Splunk, Sentinel, Elastic), EDR (CrowdStrike, Defender), SOAR (automation and orchestration), TIP (threat intel platform), vulnerability scanner, network analysis tools (Wireshark, Zeek, NetworkMiner), forensics tools (FTK Imager, Volatility, Autopsy), collaboration platform (Teams, Slack), ticketing system (ServiceNow, Jira).

quiz.question: What does SOAR stand for?
What tool is essential for network analysis?
What is Volatility used for?
What integrates SOC tools?
What is a collaboration tool?
Which best describes Day 3: SOC Tools and Stack?

topics.title: Day 4: SOC Metrics and KPIs
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: SOC Metrics and KPIs is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Define SOC metrics
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 4: SOC Metrics and KPIs and its role in SOC architecture, tiers, metrics
topics.interviewQuestion: What are key SOC metrics and KPIs?
topics.interviewAnswer: Operational: alert volume (total, per type), time to triage, time to escalate, false positive rate. Performance: mean time to detect (MTTD), mean time to respond (MTTR), mean time to contain (MTTC), mean time to recover (MTTR). Quality: analyst utilization, training completion, case documentation score.

quiz.question: What does MTTD measure?
What is a good false positive rate target?
What does MTTR measure?
What is a quality metric?
What drives metric targets?
Which best describes Day 4: SOC Metrics and KPIs?
quiz.explanation: Day 4: SOC Metrics and KPIs is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

topics.title: Day 5: SOC Design Project
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: SOC Design Project is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Design SOC
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Explain Day 5: SOC Design Project and its role in SOC architecture, tiers, metrics
topics.interviewQuestion: How do you design a SOC from scratch?
topics.interviewAnswer: Define mission and scope (what's monitored, hours of operation). Determine model (internal vs MSSP), staffing (tiers, shifts), tools (budget-driven), processes (playbooks, SLAs), facility (physical security). Create implementation roadmap: Phase 1 (foundation), Phase 2 (expansion), Phase 3 (optimization).

quiz.question: What is first step in SOC design?
What staffing model covers 24/7?
What SIEM supports cloud environments well?
What Phase 1 includes?
What is a SOC budget consideration?
Which best describes Day 5: SOC Design Project?
